sourceMovie.title: Corpse Bride
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Corpse Bride works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Corpse Bride clicked for you, Beauty and the Beast (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hotel Transylvania 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Corpse Bride clicked for you, Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ghost
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Corpse Bride clicked for you, Ghost (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Corpse Bride?
faq.answer: Try Beauty and the Beast, Hotel Transylvania 2, Ghost—each shares DNA with Corpse Bride while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Corpse Bride?
faq.answer: Corpse Bride (2005) is best described as Romance, Fantasy, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Corpse Bride worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
